methods.description: This method describes the process of selecting Alocasia species and outgroups for the study, obtaining plant material, and verifying their taxonomic identity.

steps.description: Of currently 113 Alocasia species, we here sample 71, represented by 78 plants.
steps.searchable_summary: select Alocasia species and individuals for sampling
inputs: list of currently known Alocasia species (113)

steps.description: As outgroups, we included 25 species representing 16 genera of the Pistia clade (Renner and Zhang, 2004;Cabrera et al., 2008;Cusimano et al., 2011), usually the type species of the genus name.
steps.searchable_summary: select outgroup species from the Pistia clade
inputs: list of genera/species in the Pistia clade
phylogenetic literature (Renner and Zhang, 2004; Cabrera et al., 2008; Cusimano et al., 2011)

steps.description: Leaf material came from herbarium specimens or silica-dried leaf samples (Table S1).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain plant leaf material from specified sources
inputs: Table S1 (list of species and material origin)
outputs: plant leaf material for DNA extraction

steps.description: Determination of plant material relied on comparison with herbarium material carried out by PB and LN.
steps.searchable_summary: verify taxonomic identity of plant material by comparison with herbarium specimens
outputs: verified species identity for each sample

methods.description: This method details the laboratory procedures for extracting total DNA from plant samples, amplifying specific nuclear and plastid gene regions using Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR), and sequencing the amplified DNA fragments.

steps.description: Total DNA from silica-dried leaves was extracted with the Nucleo-Spin plant kit according to the manufacturer's protocol (Macherey-Nagel, Düren, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: extract total DNA from plant tissue using a commercial kit

steps.description: Sequencing of the >2500 nucleotide (nt)-long trnK marker, amplified in one piece with the primer pair trnK-3914F (dicot) -trnK-16R (Johnson and Soltis, 1994), was problematic. Consequently, we designed new internal primers and amplified the section in four pieces: trnK-3914F -trnK-AR-alo, trnK-19F -trnK-RM-ara, trnK-FM-ara -trnK-1760R-alo, and trnK-1640F-alo -trnK-R1-mono. The new primer sequences are as follows: trnK-AR-alo 5 0 -CTC TTG AAA GAG AAG CGG ATA TAG-3 0 , trnK-19F 5 0 -TGT TCT GGC CAT ATC GCA CTA TG-3 0 , trnK-RM-ara 5 0 -AAG ATG TTG ATC GTA AAT AAG AGG-3 0 , trnK-FM-ara 5 0 -GTT TTG CTG TCA TTA TGG AAA TTC-3 0 , trnK-1760R-alo 5 0 -TAC CGC TGA AGG ATT TAT TAG GAC-3 0 , trnK-1640F-alo 5 0 -GGG ACT CAT CTT  CTG ATG AAG AAA-3 0 , trnK-R1-mono 5 0 -CAT TTT TCA TTG CAC ACG RC-3 0 .
steps.searchable_summary: amplify trnK gene region in four overlapping fragments using specific primers
inputs: trnK primers (trnK-3914F, trnK-16R, trnK-AR-alo, trnK-19F, trnK-RM-ara, trnK-FM-ara, trnK-1760R-alo, trnK-1640F-alo, trnK-R1-mono)

steps.description: PhyC was also amplified in two pieces with the newly designed primers: A20F -748R and 430F -AR: A20F: 5 0 -CAC TCA ATC CTA CAA ACT GGC-3 0 , 748R: 5 0 -ACA AGA TCC ATG ACA TTA GGT GAT T-3 0 , 430F: 5 0 CTC GTG ATG TCT GTC ACA ATA AG-3 0 and AR: 5 0 -GAA TAG CAT CCA TTT CAA CAT C-3 0 .
steps.searchable_summary: amplify phyC gene region in two overlapping fragments using specific primers

steps.description: The rpl20-rps12 intergenic spacer was amplified using the primers and PCR conditions described in Renner and Zhang (2004).
steps.searchable_summary: amplify rpl20-rps12 intergenic spacer using published primers and conditions

steps.description: The trnL intron and trnL-F intergenic spacer was amplified according to Taberlet et al. (1991) with the primer pairs c-d and e-f, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify trnL intron using published primers c-d
inputs: trnL primers (c-d, from Taberlet et al., 1991)

steps.description: The trnL intron and trnL-F intergenic spacer was amplified according to Taberlet et al. (1991) with the primer pairs c-d and e-f, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify trnL-F intergenic spacer using published primers e-f
inputs: trnL-F primers (e-f, from Taberlet et al., 1991)

steps.description: PCR products were cleaned using the NucleoSpin Ò Extract II kit (Macherey-Nagel, Dueren, Germany),
steps.searchable_summary: purify PCR products using a commercial cleanup kit

steps.description: and sequencing reactions were run on an ABI 3130 capillary sequencer (Applied Biosystems), following the manufacturers' protocols.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DNA sequencing reactions on a capillary sequencer

steps.description: Sequence editing and alignment were carried out in Sequencher 4.7 (Gene Codes, Ann Arbor, Michigan, USA) and McClade 4.0 (Maddison and Maddison, 2000).
steps.searchable_summary: edit and align DNA sequences using bioinformatics software

steps.description: New sequences generated for this study have been deposited in GenBank (accession numbers in Table S1),
steps.searchable_summary: deposit new DNA sequences into GenBank database

steps.description: and alignments for this study in TreeBASE (submission ID 12182).
steps.searchable_summary: deposit DNA sequence alignments into TreeBASE database
